name: White Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Finely crush the digestive biscuits in a food processor.
2. Melt 4oz (100g) butter and stir in the biscuit crumbs until thoroughly mixed.
3. Spoon the mixture into an 8in (20.5cm) springform cake tin, pressing evenly over the base and sides.
4. Freeze for 30 minutes.
5. In a bowl set over simmering water, melt 6oz (150g) white chocolate and 6fl oz (170ml) double cream until smooth.
6. In another clean bowl, whisk together soft cheese, sugar, flour, and vanilla extract. Whisk in eggs one at a time.
7. Pour the melted chocolate and cream over the biscuit base, then pour the remaining butter, cream, and plain chocolate mixture into the cheesecake batter, ensuring it is smooth.
8. Bake at 350°F (180°C, Gas 4) for 45 minutes, then cover with foil and cook for a further 15-20 minutes.
9. Cool, then chill for 1 hour. Remove from the tin.
10. Melt remaining white chocolate, spread onto a baking sheet, and let it harden.
11. Heat remaining butter, cream, and plain chocolate until melted and smooth.
12. Pour over the chilled cheesecake and smooth with a palette knife. Chill for an additional hour.
13. Use a sharp knife to draw across the set white chocolate to form curls, which can be used to decorate the cheesecake.
14. Serve in slices.
